Triaging 11 tickets (65103 listed twice). Here are the results.

---

**#65103** — Open PR in wordpress-develop is **closed**; the fix has been moved to a Gutenberg PR (#78136) because the affected file is auto-generated from Gutenberg. westonruter asked fabiankaegy to test the Gutenberg PR on 2026-05-11. No actionable PR exists in wordpress-develop — a worker dispatched here can't fix it without touching Gutenberg.

**#65245** — Two recent comments (ocean90, jonsurrell) agree the bug lives on meta.trac.wordpress.org (API returns bad encoding), not on core. Likely heading toward wontfix/invalid in core trac. No PR.

**#65141** — Originally fixed in [62284]/[62285], then reopened by TobiasBg with substantive follow-up (`-4px` margin also needs reverting). New PR #11689 is open, APPROVED by audrasjb, CI green, multiple recent test reports. audrasjb explicitly confirms PR resolves the ticket ("I think we can go with PR11689"). Title doesn't reference ticket number verbatim but committer confirmation is explicit.

**#65051** — PR #11530 open, CI success, title matches ticket summary verbatim. Companion test PR #11673 also open, CI success.

**#65233** — Fixed in [62357], reopened explicitly to request backport to 7.0 branch. All post-reopen activity is bookkeeping plus tester reports confirming the existing fix works — no new substantive issues. Backport is committer-only.

**#65218** — Single open PR #11798, CI success, recently updated, no review changes requested. PR title references "Updates screen" and topic matches, but no explicit ticket reference or commenter confirmation — scope match is inference-based.

**#65214** — Open PR #11785, but sabernhardt's comment:3 raises substantive concerns about the proposed wording ("backend editor" isn't used in user-facing strings; suggests alternative wording; flags interaction with #64904). Author hasn't responded. Wording approach is in flux.

**#62722** — Status `new` with keyword `close`. Dev-chat consensus (per comment:17) was that direct-access ABSPATH errors are a server-config concern, not a core priority. Single PR is closed (2025-08-19). Multiple tickets have been merged in as duplicates without movement. Awaiting committer to close as wontfix.

**#24552** — Open since 2013. Two recent reproduction reports (comments 9, 10) confirm the originally reported behavior no longer occurs on trunk; comment:10 explicitly suggests `worksforme`. No PR. Awaiting committer to close.

**#64696** — Partial mitigation landed in [62099] but the architectural question is unresolved. Multiple competing approaches (custom table, transients, hybrid, post-meta JIT invalidation) with active multi-author disagreement across 130+ comments. Punted from 7.0 → 7.1 (comment:173). Six open PRs, multiple authors. Classic needs-architectural-discussion case.
